original: Boniface III begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Medieval Iconoclastic Controversy ends as a council in Constantinople formally reinstates the veneration of icons in churches
French troops under Gaston de Foix occupy Brescia in Italy
Jews of Tyrnau, Hungary (Trnava, Czechia) are expelled
Spanish troops plunder Krommenie, Wormerveer, and Jisp in the Netherlands
Francis, Duke of Anjou, gains the title Duke of Brabant, the hereditary sovereign of the Dutch United Provinces
Peruvian stratovolcano Huaynaputina explodes in the most violent eruption in South American recorded history
Battle of Smolensk: King Wladyslaw IV of Poland beats Russians [NS=Mar 1]
Dutch Republic and England sign Treaty of Westminster, ending the Third Anglo-Dutch War; North American colony of New Netherland returned to British [OS date, 02-09 NS]
Last day of Julian calendar in Denmark
US Congress accepts Ohio's bid for statehood, but neglects to ratify their constitution; oversight no corrected until1953
British squadron under Admiral Duckworth attempts to force passage of Dardanelles
Franz Grillparzer's  stage drama "Konig Ottokars Gluck" premieres in Vienna at the Burgtheater
1st practical US coal-burning locomotive makes 1st trial run in Pennsylvania
Texas state government formally installed in Austin
The Phi Kappa Psi fraternity is founded at Jefferson College in Canonsburg, Pennsylvania
Tin-type camera patented by Hamilton Smith, Gambier, Ohio
Knights of Pythias form 1st lodge in Washington, D.C. (12 members)
US Assay Office in Boise, Idaho authorized
Kansas becomes 1st state to prohibit all alcoholic beverages
"Enigma Outbreak" of over 60 tornadoes in Mississippi, Alabama, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Kentucky and Indiana kill hundreds, if not over a thousand (hence the "enigma") people
Inaugural meeting of the Women's Institute at Stoney Creek, Ontario, set at the suggestion of Adelaide Hoodless, the organization will rapidly spread around the world
British troops occupy Hlangwane, Natal
1st prize inserted into a Cracker Jack box
Riccardo Zandonai's opera "Francesco da Rimini" premieres in Turin
British fleet opens fire on Dardanelles coast
WWI: Germany sinks American Merchant ship Evelyn with mine in the North Sea
Netherlands joins League of Nations
Ed Wynn becomes 1st talent to sign as a radio entertainer
Philip Barry's play "You & I" premieres in NYC
General strike against British occupiers in Shanghai
Canada retains the Olympic ice hockey title when they rout Switzerland, 13-0 in the final round in St. Moritz; Canadian left-wing Dave Trottier top scores with 15 points

Gillis Grafström of Sweden wins his 3rd consecutive Olympic men’s figure skating gold medal at St. Moritz; one of only 2 athletes to win gold at both Winter & Summer Games (won figure skating Antwerp 1920)
II Winter Olympic Games close at St Moritz, Switzerland
World champion French team Andrée Joly and Pierre Brunet win the Olympic pairs figure skating gold medal at the St. Moritz Winter Games; the couple marry in 1929 and take 2nd straight gold in 1932
Medical diathermy machine 1st used in Schenectady, NY
US contract air mail service canceled, replaced by US army for 6 months
Soviet arctic ice research station North Pole 1 evacuated, Denmark
Nazi police attacked & driven away from Koco, Amsterdam (by young Jews)
An estimated 150 Japanese warplanes attack the Australian city of Darwin
Bill Longson beats Managoff & Sandor Szabo, to become wrestling champ
Dutch actors protest obligatory membership of Culture Chamber
Japanese troops land on Timor
NY Yankees announce 5,000 uniformed soldiers admitted free at each of their upcoming home games
German tanks under Major General Karl Buelowius attack Kasserine Pass, Tunisia
823 British bombers attack Berlin
U-264 sinks off Ireland
980 Japanese soldiers reportedly killed by crocodiles in two days on Ramree Island, Burma
NY Giants outfielder Danny Gardella is the first major leaguer to announce he is jumping to the "outlaw" Mexican Baseball League for a fee of $10,000
CBS radio premiere of Villa-Lobos' "Bachianas Brasilieras No 3"
Mass arrests of communists in India
Groundbreaking ceremony held for Mississippi Vocational College (later Mississippi Valley State University)
First Indochina War: French offensive at Hanoi
Georgia approves US 1st literature censorship board
William Inge's "Picnic" premieres in NYC
South East Asia Collective Defense Treaty goes into effect
Doo-wop vocal group The Five Satins record leader and songwriter Fred Parris's tune "In the Still of the Nite" in the basement of St. Bernadette's Church in New Haven, Connecticut
Britain, Turkey, and Greece sign an agreement granting Cyprus independence
Gabon adopts its constitution
USAF rocket-powered rail sled attains Mach 4.1 (4970 kph), at Holloman AFB, New Mexico
Bil Keane's "Family Circus" cartoon strip debuts
Protest strike in Poznan, Poland
Swedish cross country skier Sixten Jernberg follows his 50k gold medal in Cortina d'Ampezzo (1956) with victory in 30k event at the Squaw Valley Winter Olympics
Three time Canadian world champions Barbara Wagner and Robert Paul win the pairs figure skating Olympic gold medal at Squaw Valley, California
Albania disavows Chinese "Revisionism"
Dutchman Henk van der Grift becomes world champ all-round skater
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
UK flies ½ ton of Beatles wigs to the US
NFL adds 6th official - the line judge
Stien Kaiser becomes world champion lady's skater
Children's educational TV program "Mister Rogers' Neighborhood" debuts on NET (now PBS)
First statewide teachers' strike in the US begins in Florida [note: a two-day walkout in Utah in 1963 is considered first by some]
USSR launches Sputnik 52 & Molniya 1-13 communications satellite
NZ cricketer Glenn Turner carries his bat for 223* vs West Indies at Kingston
The Asama-Sanso hostage standoff begins in Japan
"Tie a Yellow Ribbon Round the Ole Oak Tree" single released by Dawn featuring Tony Orlando (Billboard Song of the Year 1973)
Frente Polisario forms Democratic Republic of Sahara
19th Grammy Awards: "This Masquerade" by George Benson, and Starland Vocal Band win
A's sell pitcher Paul Lindblad to the Rangers for $400,000
Doug Walters scores 250 v NZ, 217 stand for 7th wicket with Gilmour
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Right wing Rod Gilbert scores a goal and adds an assist in a 5-2 defeat at the NY Islanders to become first player in NY Rangers history (11th in NHL) to score 1,000 career points
Space shuttle Enterprise makes 1st Test flight atop a 747 jetliner
Swedish alpine skier Ingemar Stenmark wins the giant slalom from Andreas Wenzel of Liechtenstein at the Lake Placid Winter Olympics; also takes slalom gold
Ulrich Wehling of East Germany wins his third consecutive Olympic gold medal in the Nordic combined event at Lake Placid; winner Sapporo (1972), Innsbruck (1976)
Washington beats Montreal, 3-1 to end the Canadiens' 34 game unbeaten streak against Capitals; Caps 0-32-2 against Montreal since entering NHL in 1974
Hanneke Jagersma installed as the 1st Communist mayor in the Netherlands
Sharie Langford, California, sets women's bowling series record of 853
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Vladimir Salnikov (USSR) sets 400 m free style swimming record
1st brother combo to win Gold & Silver in same event at Olympics (Phil & Steve Mahre-Slalom)
Soviet Union wins its 6th Olympic ice hockey gold medal with a 2-0 win over silver medallists Czechoslovakia at the Sarajevo Winter Games
Swedish cross country skier Thomas Wassberg wins 50k event at the Sarajevo Winter Olympics; his second gold of the Games (4 x 10k relay) and 3rd of his career (15k Lake Placid 1980)
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
XIV Winter Olympic Games close in Sarajevo, Yugoslavia
150 killed when a Spanish jetliner crashes approaching Bilbao, Spain
M of Amsterdam declares bankruptcy
British soap opera "Eastenders" premieres on the BBC
Canned & bottled Cherry Coke introduced by Coca-Cola
Mickey Mouse welcomed in China
William Schroeder is 1st artificial heart patient to leave hospital He spends 15 minutes outside Humana Hospital in Louisville, Kentucky
King Hussein of Jordan severs ties with PLO
US Senate ratifies UN's anti-genocide convention 37 years later
USSR launches Mir space station into Earth orbit
Minnesota sheriff's office arrest FBI's most wanted, Thomas G. Harrelson
Helga Arendt, Silke-Beate Knoll, Mechthild Kluth, Gisela Kinzel walk indoor female world record 4x200m (1:32.55)
Edgar Bowers wins the Bollingen Prize in Poetry
Police kill 8 demonstrators for multi party system in Nepal
Soyuz TM-9 lands
American porn producer Jim Mitchell found guilty of killing his brother Artie
In a blockbuster NHL trade, Pittsburgh Penguins send Mark Recchi, Brian Benning & LA's 1st round 1992 pick (Jason Bowen) to the Philadelphia Flyers for Kjell Samuelsson, Rick Tocchet & Ken Wregget
Professional Spring Football League announces end of operations, 10 days before the scheduled season opener; 10 teams had players in camp and practicing, and rosters cut down to 60 players
"Happy Nation" debut studio album by Swedish pop group Ace of Base is released
37th Daytona 500: Sterling Marlin wins for 2nd straight year; 3rd driver to achieve back-to-back titles (Richard Petty,Cale Yarborough)
British composer Michael Tippett's last major work "Rose Lake," premieres at Barbican Hall in London, with the London Symphony led by Colin Davis
Kenneth Koch wins Bollingen Prize for One Train (1994) and On The Great Atlantic Rainway: Selected Poems 1950-1988 (1994)
Linford Christie runs world record 200m indoor (20.25 sec) and European record 60m indoor (6.47 sec)
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in York, Pennsylvania on WQXA 105.7 FM
FCC makes available 311 for non-emergency calls and 711 for hearing or speech-impaired emergency calls
After being eliminated by the Czech Republic, the US ice hockey team trashes three dorm rooms at the Olympic Village in Nagano, Japan
Soyuz TM-26 lands
An Oklahoma City bombing museum is dedicated at the Oklahoma City National Memorial
NASA's Mars Odyssey space probe begins to map the surface of Mars using its thermal emission imaging system
Nazi-hunter Simon Wiesenthal of Austria, is awarded an honorary British knighthood in recognition of a "lifetime of service to humanity."
48th Daytona 500: Jimmie Johnson driving from Hendrick Motorsports wins 203 lap race; goes on to win his first championship
Blogging website Tumblr is founded by David Karp in New York
Three Salvadoran deputies to the Central American Parliament and their driver are murdered in Guatemala
Serzh Sargsyan wins the Armenian Presidential Election
Toshiba announces its formal recall of its HD DVD video format, ending the format war with Sony's Blu-ray Disc
44 people killed in prison brawl in Apocada, Mexico, between two rival drug cartels
12 people are killed and 11 are injured after a Yemeni Air Force plane crashes in Sana'a
After winning the combined gold in Turin (2006), American alpine skier Ted Ligety has to wait 8 years for his next Olympic success, gold in the giant slalom in Sochi
Canadian team Kaillie Humphries and Heather Moyes win their second consecutive Olympic 2-woman bobsleigh gold medal at the Sochi Winter Games
Czech speed skater Martina Sáblíková retains her Olympic 5,000m title (Vancouver 2010) in Sochi; also Vancouver 3,000m gold
Death toll in Ukraine reaches 26 after Government crackdown on protesters
Norwegian biathletes Ole Einar Bjørndalen (sprint gold) and
At least 17 killed when a garbage mound collapses in Maputo, Mozambique
Canadian team of Justin Kripps & Alexander Kopacz dead-heat in 3:16.86 with Germans Francesco Friedrich & Thorsten Margis for the 2-man bobsleigh gold medal at the Pyeongchang Winter Olympics
Nigeria says 110 girls missing, presumed kidnapped by Boko Haram after attack on school in Dapchi, Yobe state
Syrian government forces bombard Gouta in deadliest day in 3 years, killing over 100 civilians
Four-time MLB All-Star at 3B Manny Machado agrees to the biggest free-agent contract in American sports history, a 10 year deal worth $300 million with the San Diego Padres
New York city bans hair discrimination, to limit racial stereotyping
Vatican confirms secret church guidelines for children of priests
German gunman opens fire in a bar in Hanau, Germany, killing nine in a racially motivated attack
In a softening of 4-year WADA ban on Russia from all international sport, Russia to compete under acronym "ROC" after name of the Russian Olympic Committee
Alexander Bolshunov, representing Russian Olympic Committee, wins men's 50k freestyle cross country gold in Beijing to become only 2nd man to achieve 30k/50k double at a Winter Olympics
65th Daytona 500: Ricky Stenhouse Jr. wins for JTG Daugherty Racing in double overtime and under caution over a record 212 laps; former NBA player Brad Daugherty first Black car owner to win the race
Brazilian state of Sao Paulo hit by flooding and landslides after heavy rain, with at least 44 killed and 800 homeless

Türkiye ends rescue efforts in all but Kahramanmaras and Hatay, two weeks after the devastating earthquakes that stuck in the south-east and in northern Syria, killing between 59,488–62,013 people and leaving an estimated 1.5 million people homeless

66th Daytona 500: William Byron takes the lead on a restart with 4 laps to go and then wins after Ross Chastain and Austin Cindric crash to bring out a race-ending caution; Hendrick Motorsports record-tying 9th win
Brightest known object in the universe identified as a quasar, 500 trillion x brighter than the sun, powered by a supermassive black hole that devours a sun-sized mass every day
Investigation into 2021 assassination of Haitian President Jovenel Moise charges his wife, the former PM and ex-chief of police

Fastest Atlantic crossing record-holding American ocean liner SS United States departs Philadelphia, Pennsylvania under tow on her final voyage before being scuttled to become the world's largest artificial reef
Battle of Lugdunum [Battle of Lyon]:Lucius Septimius Severus' army defeats Clodius Albinus at Lyon
Roman EmperorConstantius IIcloses all pagan temples
Consecration ofHenry VII's Lady Chapel, part of Westminster Abbey, called "one of the most perfect buildings ever erected in England"

Having already inherited the throne of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth through his mother, Catherine Jagellonica of Poland,Sigismund IIIof the House of Vasa is crowned King of Sweden, succeeding his father John III of Sweden.
English "Happy Parliament", the final Parliament ofKing James Iopens at Westminster
George Frideric Handel's opera "Alexander's Feast" premieres at the Covent Garden Theatre, London
French astronomerCharles Messieradds M46-M49 to his catalog (galactic clusters in Puppis & Hydra & galaxy in Virgo)
The Peace of Tolentino : PopePius VIcedes Papal territories of Avignon, Venaissin, Ferrara, Bologna, and the Romagna to France
US Vice PresidentAaron Burrarrested in Alabama for treason; later acquitted
British explorer William Smith discovers the uninhabited South Shetland Islands, near Antarctica, claims them in the name of KingGeorge III

Will Keith Kelloggjoins Charles D. Bolin in founding the Battle Creek Toasted Corn Flake Company, now the multinational food manufacturer Kellogg's, after falling out with his brother over development credit and wanting to add sugar to cereal
English premiere ofRichard Strauss' opera "Elektra"
Typhoid Mary[Mary Mallon] is freed from her first periods of forced isolation and goes on to cause several further outbreaks of typhoid in the New York area
Mexican GeneralVictoriano Huertaseizes power after a 10 day coup d'état in Mexico City with US support, deposing the elected PresidentFrancisco I. Madero
Pan-African Congress, organized by American civil rights activistW.E.B. Du Boisis held at the Grand Hotel in Paris, France
Jean Sibelius' 6th Symphony premieres
William Faulknercompletes his novel "Light in August"
Hermann Göringbans all Catholic newspapers in Cologne, claiming that Catholics illegally engage in politics
Clifford Odets' "Awake & Sing" premieres in NYC
Manuel Azañabecomes Prime Minister of Spain for the second time
FDRorders the detention and internment of all Japanese Americans on the West Coast
Tommy Dorseyand his orchestra record "I'll Take Tallulah"
First Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded toEzra Pound

American defending championDick Buttonbecomes 1st figure-skater to land a triple jump in competition; performs triple loop in Olympic free skate in Oslo; wins gold medal ahead of Austria’s Helmut Seibt
Carl Perkinsleaves Sun Records for Columbia Records
"The Feminine Mystique" byBetty Friedan, widely credited as the start of second-wave feminism, is published
Robert Frostwins Bollingen Prize in Poetry
USSR informsJFKit is withdrawing several thousand troops from Cuba

Paul McCartney, his girlfriend Jane Asher, andRingo Starrand his wife Maureen travel to India, joiningJohn Lennon,George Harrisonand their wives to study Transcendental Meditation for two months withMaharishi Mahesh Yogiat his ashram in Rishikesh; Ringo only stays for 2 weeks
AL Cy Young Award winnerDenny McLainsuspended for bookmaking
British TV chat show "Parkinson" debuts on BBC1 presented byMichael Parkinson
Paul McCartneyreleases single "Another Day" in UK
Progressive rockband Yesrelease their break-through third studio album "The Yes Album"

American singerHarry Nilsson's single "Without You" begins a 4 week run at the top of the charts. The song was originally written and performed by the English band Badfinger in 1970, and would reach the top ten again with Mariah Carey's 1994 cover.
Manfred Mann's Earth Band's cover ofBruce Springsteen's song "Blinded By The Light" goes to #1
20th Daytona 500:Bobby Allisontakes lead with 11 laps remaining; lowest starting position (33rd) for winner until 2007
American speed skaterEric Heidenwins 1,000m in Olympic record 1:15.18; third of record 5 gold medal sweep of events at Lake Placid
Ian Bothamscores a century and takes 13 wickets in the Jubilee cricket Test at Bombay
George Harrisonis ordered to pay ABKCO Music $587,000 for "subconscious plagiarism" of his song "My Sweet Lord" from Ronnie Mack's song "He's So Fine"
Baseball pitcherFernando Valenzuelawins his salary arbitration of $1 million
26th Daytona 500:Cale Yarboroughwins for 2nd consecutive year and 4th time in career; last-lap pass to beatDarrell Waltrip
Less than a month after re-signing, Oakland A's pitcherVida Blueretires
USPresident Reaganlifts trade boycott against Poland
31st Daytona 500:Darrell Waltripwins when Hendrick Racing team decides to use fuel strategy, being the only car not to pit in the closing laps
Geffen Records releases "Night Ride Home", Canadian singer-songwriterJoni Mitchell's 14th studio album
Dramatic mini-series "A Woman of Independent Means" starringSally Field, premieres on NBC-TV, airing 6 hours over 3 nights
Bernie Sandersannounces he is running for President for a second time
US Secretary of StateAntony Blinken, visiting the earthquake-affected regions of southern Turkey, pledges $100 million in aid
Thomas Edisonis granted a patent for his cylinder phonograph
Four-year old Charlotte May Pierstorff mailed by train from Grangeville, Idaho to her grandparents’ house 73 miles away in most famous 'child in the post' instance
US 5th Fleet launches invasion of Iwo Jima against the Japanese with 30,000 US Marines
Carolus Clusius [Charles de L'Ecluse], Artois botanist, developed the tulip, potato, and chestnut for cultivation, born in Arras, County of Artois (d. 1609)
Melchior Klesl, Austrian cardinal and statesman, tried to create religious tolerance, born in Vienna, Austria (d. 1630)
Pieter de Carpentier, Dutch administrator (Governor-General of Dutch East-Indies 1623-27, Gulf of Carpentaria named after him), born in Antwerp (d. 1659)
Andries de Graeff, Dutch leader during Dutch Golden Age (Mayor of Amsterdam 1657-71), born in Amsterdam (d. 1678)
Shivaji Bhonsle [Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj], Indian warrior and founder of the Maratha Empire (disputed birth date; 1630-02-19 accepted by Indian government), born at Shivneri fort (d. 1680)
Friedrich Hoffmann, German physician and chemist, first to investigate carbon monoxide poisoning, born in Halle, Archbishopric of Magdeburg (d. 1742)
Charles-Hubert Gervais, French baroque composer, born at the Palais Royal in Paris, France (d. 1744)
David Garrick, English actor, producer and writer (Aboan-Oroonoko), born in Hereford, England (d. 1779)
Tiphaigne de la Roche, French fantasy writer with early sci-fi themes ("Zamar, Delegated to the Moon by Amilec"), born in Montebourg, France (d. 1774)

Nicolas Baudin, French explorer, led cartographic survey of the coast of Australia, born in St-Pierre-de-Ré, France (d. 1803)

Lauro Rossi, Italian composer (I falsi monetari), born in Macerata, Kingdom of Italy (d. 1885)
William III, King of the Netherlands (1849-90) and the last male King until 2013, born in the Palace of the Nation, Brussels, United Kingdom of the Netherlands (d. 1890)

Emánuel Moór, Hungarian composer, pianist, and inventor of musical instruments, born in Kecskemét, Hungary (d. 1931)
Sven Hedin, Swedish scientist and explorer who travelled and mapped Central Asia (Central Asia Atlas), born in Stockholm, Sweden (d. 1952)

Hendrik Spruit, Dutch conductor and composer, born in Utrecht, Netherlands (d. 1998)

Roberto Sánchez Vilella, Puerto Rico politician (Governor of Puerto Rico, 1965-69; Secretary of State of Puerto Rico, 1953-65; Mayor of San Juan, 1945-46), born in Mayaguez,  Puerto Rico (d. 1997)

Frank Glazer, American pianist (Satie), composer and teacher (Eastman School of Music, 1965-80), born in Chester, Wisconsin (d. 2015)
John Freeman, British politician (Labour), ambassador, TV host and executive (London Weekend Television), born in London, England (d. 2014)
Carson McCullers, American novelist (The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter), born in Columbus, Georgia (d. 1967)
Richard "Dick" Emery, British comedian and actor (Yellow Submarine, Loot, Baby Love), born in London, England (d. 1983)
Fay McKenzie, American actress (The Party; Sierra Sue), and singer, born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2019)
C. Z. Guest, American socialite and fashion icon, born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 2003)

Jaan Kross, Estonian writer (The Czar's Madman), born in Tallinn, Estonia (d. 2007)
Andries Treurnicht [Dr No], South African politician (founder of the South African Conservative Party), born in Piketberg, South Africa (d. 1993)
Claude Pascal, French composer, educator (Paris Conservatoire, 1952-87), and music critic (Le Figaro, 1969-79), born in Paris, France (d. 2017)
Beant Singh, Indian politician (Chief Minister of Punjab), born in Patiala, Punjab (d. 1995)
Fredell Lack, American concert violinist, and teacher (University of Houston, 1959-2009), born in Tulsa, Oklahoma (d. 2017)
Josef Matěj, Czech trombonist, and composer (Monograms), born in Brušperk, Czechoslovakia (d. 1992)
Donald Lybbert, American composer (Zap; Lines for the Fallen), and educator (Hunter College, 1954-80), born in Cresco, Iowa (d. 1981)
Bruce Norris, American Hockey Hall of Fame executive (owner Detroit Red Wings 1952-82), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1986)
Jindřich Feld, Czech composer (Concerto for Flute and Orchestra), born in Prague, Czechoslovakia (d. 2007)
